title: arnica louiseana subsp. frigida
intro: Arnica louiseana subsp. frigida, also known as mountain arnica, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to reduce swelling, pain, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Arnica louiseana subsp. frigida include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it a popular treatment for bruises, sprains, and other injuries. Arnica preparations can be made into topical creams, ointments, and tinctures, which can be applied directly to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
